data.content:
Advisory multi-step process
A structured approach used by an agency to evaluate potential offerors for an acquisition.

Agency heads
Human individuals responsible for source selection in acquisitions.

Tradeoff process
A process that involves considering factors other than the lowest price or highest technical rating when awarding contracts.

Agency
The agency evaluates the process.

Contracting officer
Agency heads designate the contracting officer as the source selection authority.<SEP>Individual responsible for the exchange of information with potential offerors.<SEP>A person responsible for listing attachments in Section J.

Government
When it may be in the best interest of the Government to consider award to other than the lowest priced offeror or other than the highest technically rated offeror.<SEP>When using a tradeoff process, the Government may consider factors other than cost or price.<SEP>The perceived benefits of the higher priced proposal shall merit the additional cost.<SEP>Allows the Government to accept other than the lowest priced proposal.<SEP>Government entity responsible for publicizing requirements and soliciting information.<SEP>Companies, institutions, government bodies, groups that participate in oral presentations and make source selection decisions.

Inquiry focal point
Contracting officer serves as the focal point for inquiries after solicitation release.

Evaluation team
Agency heads establish an evaluation team for acquisitions.

Evaluation factors
The solicitation shall state whether all evaluation factors other than cost or price, when combined, are significantly more important than, approximately equal to, or significantly less important than cost or price.<SEP>All evaluation factors and significant subfactors that will affect contract award and their relative importance shall be clearly stated in the solicitation.

Proposal exchanges
Contracting officer controls exchanges with offerors after receipt of proposals.

Source selection strategy
Agency heads approve the source selection strategy before solicitation release.

Contract award
Contracting officer awards the contract(s) after receipt of proposals.

Solicitation requirements
Agency heads ensure consistency among solicitation requirements.

Solicitation
Contracting officer serves as the focal point for inquiries from actual or prospective offerors.<SEP>Documents used to solicit proposals from potential offerors.

15.303
Numerical value related to responsibilities.

currency
A medium of exchange used to facilitate the sale, purchase, or trade of goods and services

relevance
The importance, applicability, or significance of something in a particular context

Data
Numerical value related to responsibilities.

source of information
The origin or provider of information

context of data
The circumstances or setting in which data is collected or used

trends in contractors performance
Patterns or general directions in the performance of contractors over time

data.entity_name: comparative assessment of past performance information
data.content:
comparative assessment of past performance information
A comparison of past performance data to evaluate current or future performance

responsibility determination
The process of deciding who is accountable for a particular task or outcome

solicitation
A formal request for proposals or bids<SEP>Creative or informational works that provide offerors with information to prepare for oral presentations.

Offerors
Contracting officer controls exchanges with offerors in accordance with 15.306 after receipt of proposals.

approach for evaluating past performance
A method or strategy for assessing past performance

offerors
Individuals or entities that submit bids or proposals in response to a solicitation<SEP>Companies, institutions, government bodies, groups that are required to prepare and present oral presentations.

Advisory boards
Agency heads consider recommendations of advisory boards.

relevant performance history
Past performance that is pertinent or applicable to the current situation

solicitation authority
The entity or individual with the power to issue a solicitation

source selection authority
The entity or individual responsible for selecting the source of a contract or award

data.entity_name: relevance of similar past performance information
data.content:
relevance of similar past performance information
The significance or applicability of past performance data that is similar to the current situation

Responsibilities
Numerical value related to responsibilities.

predecessor companies
Companies that previously held a position or responsibility before the current entity

key personnel
Important or influential individuals within an organization

subcontractors
Entities that provide goods or services under a contract

offeror without relevant past performance
An individual or entity that has not demonstrated past performance that is relevant to the current situation

Contract(s)
Contracting officer awards the contract(s) after receipt of proposals.

subcontracting plan goals
Objectives related to subcontracting, particularly for small disadvantaged business concerns

joint ventures
Business arrangements where two or more parties come together to undertake a specific project or business activity

contracting officer
An individual responsible for the administration of contracts

Best value source
Agency heads select the source with the best value to the Government.

Proposal
The objective of source selection is to select the proposal that represents the best value.<SEP>The proposal that represents the best value is selected through the source selection objective.

Source selection objective
The objective of source selection is to select the proposal that represents the best value.<SEP>The proposal that represents the best value is selected through the source selection objective.

Contracting Officer
A person responsible for the contracting process in government acquisitions.<SEP>An individual responsible for issuing solicitations to potential sources in accordance with specified policies and procedures.<SEP>An individual responsible for contracting activities.

data.entity_name: Submission, modification, revision, and withdrawal of proposals
data.content:
Submission, modification, revision, and withdrawal of proposals
A process involving the submission, revision, and withdrawal of proposals.

Non-Cost Evaluation Factors
The quality of the product or service shall be addressed in every source selection through consideration of one or more non-cost evaluation factors such as past performance, compliance with solicitation requirements, technical excellence, management capability, personnel qualifications, and prior experience (10 U.S.C. 3206(c)(1)(A) and 41 U.S.C. 3306(c)(1)(A)).

Past Performance
Past performance shall be evaluated in all source selections for negotiated competitive acquisitions expected to exceed the simplified acquisition threshold.

Price or Cost
The contracting officer shall consider price or cost as one of the factors in the selection decision for each order placed under the contract.

Small Business Participation
Past performance need not be evaluated if the contracting officer documents the reason past performance is not an appropriate evaluation factor for the acquisition.

Quality of Product or Service
The quality of the product or service shall be addressed in every source selection through consideration of one or more non-cost evaluation factors such as past performance, compliance with solicitation requirements, technical excellence, management capability, personnel qualifications, and prior experience (10 U.S.C. 3206(c)(1)(A) and 41 U.S.C. 3306(c)(1)(A)).

Source Selection
Past performance shall be evaluated in all source selections for negotiated competitive acquisitions expected to exceed the simplified acquisition threshold.

Contracting officers
Individuals responsible for issuing RFPs and authorizing receipt of proposals, modifications, or revisions by facsimile.

Solicitations
Past performance need not be evaluated if the contracting officer documents the reason past performance is not an appropriate evaluation factor for the acquisition.

RFPs
Requests for proposals used in acquisitions.

Facsimile
Procedure for transmitting documents electronically.

Proposals
Documents submitted by offerors in response to RFPs.

Multiple-Award Contracts
The exception in paragraph (c)(1)(ii)(A) of this section shall not apply to solicitations for multiple-award contracts that provide for sole source orders pursuant to section 8(a) of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 637 (a)).

Content
Letter RFPs may be used in sole source acquisitions and other appropriate circumstances.

Government office
The Government office designated in the solicitation is the entity to which proposals must be submitted by the specified time.

Letter RFPs
Letter RFPs may be used in sole source acquisitions and other appropriate circumstances.<SEP>A solicitation method that should be as complete as possible and include specific details such as RFP number and date, contracting officer's contact information, type of contract, quantity and description of items, delivery dates, certifications, contract terms, instructions to offerors, evaluation criteria, proposal due date, and other relevant information.<SEP>A solicitation method that should be as complete as possible and include specific details such as RFP number and date, contracting officer's contact information, type of contract, quantity<SEP>A solicitation method used in sole source acquisitions and other appropriate circumstances, not relieving the contracting officer from complying with other FAR requirements.

Compliance
Use of a letter RFP does not relieve the contracting officer from complying with other FAR requirements.

Circumstances
Letter RFPs may be used in sole source acquisitions and other appropriate circumstances.

joint venture
A business arrangement between two or more parties to undertake a specific project or activity, often for the purpose of sharing resources and expertise.

proposals
The source selection authority may reject all proposals received in response to a solicitation if doing so is in the best interest of the Government.

This subpart
This subpart prescribes policies and procedures for selection of a source or sources in competitive negotiated acquisitions.

prescribes policies and procedures
This subpart prescribes policies and procedures for selection of a source or sources in competitive negotiated acquisitions.

offeror
The source selection records shall include an assessment of each offeror’s ability to accomplish the technical requirements and a summary, matrix, or quantitative ranking of each technical proposal.

support contractor personnel
Restrictions on the use of support contractor personnel in proposal evaluation are specified in 37.203 (d).

Amending the solicitation
The process of modifying a solicitation document when the Government changes its requirements or terms and conditions.

small business concern
Solicitations must be structured to give offers from small business concerns the highest rating for the evaluation factors in 15.304 (c)(3)(ii) and (c)(4).

Part I-The Schedule
A section of a contract that outlines the schedule for the contracting officer to prepare the contract Schedule.

Section A, Solicitation/contract form
A section of a contract that outlines the schedule for the contracting officer to prepare the contract Schedule.

All parties receiving the solicitation
All parties must be informed of amendments issued before the deadline.

Oral presentations
A form of communication used in the acquisition process to provide information, facilitate dialogue, and potentially replace written information.

All offerors that have not been eliminated from the competition
Only offerors still in the competition should receive amendments issued after the deadline.
data.entity_name: All offerors that have not been eliminated from the competition

data.content:
Issuing activity
Each amendment should include the name and address of the issuing activity.

Industry or small business conferences
Conferences where techniques to promote early exchanges of information are discussed.

Public hearings
Hearings where techniques to promote early exchanges of information are discussed.

Market research
Research activities as described in part 10 to promote early exchanges of information.

One-on-one meetings with potential offerors
Meetings between contracting officers and potential offerors to discuss contract terms and conditions.

Potential offerors
Entities substantially involved with potential contract terms and conditions.

Presolicitation notices
Notices used to publicize the Government’s requirement or solicit information from industry.

Draft RFPs
Drafts of Requests for Proposals used to publicize the Government’s requirement or solicit information from industry.

RFIs
Requests for Information used to obtain market information or capabilities for planning purposes.

Presolicitation or preproposal conferences
Conferences used to publicize the Government’s requirement or solicit information from industry.

Site visits
Visits to potential offeror sites to promote early exchanges of information.

Special notices of procurement matters
Notices used to publicize the Government’s requirement or solicit information from industry.

Electronic notices
Electronic notices used to publicize the Government’s requirement or solicit information from industry.

Information
Information pertaining to areas such as an offeror’s capability, past performance, work plans or approaches, staffing resources, transition plans, or sample tasks may be suitable for oral presentations.

data.entity_name: General information about agency mission needs and future requirements
data.content:
General information about agency mission needs and future requirements
Information about agency mission needs and future requirements disclosed to potential offerors.

Information exchange
The process of sharing information between the Government and potential offerors.

data.entity_name: Techniques to promote early exchanges of information
data.content:
Techniques to promote early exchanges of information
Techniques discussed at various events to promote early exchanges of information.

Incidental deliverables
Deliverables such as manuals and reports that are not the primary focus of the contract.

Section C
<(c) Section C, Description/specifications/statement of work. Include any description or specifications needed in addition to Section B (see part 11, Describing Agency Needs).>

Best value continuum
An abstract idea representing the optimal balance between cost, performance, and risk in the context of negotiated acquisitions.

Other
<(c) Section C, Description/specifications/statement of work. Include any description or specifications needed in addition to Section B (see part 11, Describing Agency Needs).>

Section D
<(d) Section D, Packaging and marking. Provide packaging, packing, preservation, and marking requirements, if any.><SEP><(d) Section D, Packaging and marking. Provide packaging, packing, preservation, and marking requirements, if any

Source selection approaches
With varying levels of requirement definiteness, development work, and performance risk.<SEP>With varying importance of cost or price.

Section E
<(e) Section E, Inspection and acceptance. Include inspection, acceptance, quality assurance, and reliability requirements (see part 46, Quality Assurance).>

Section F
<(f) Section F, Deliveries or performance. Specify the requirements for time, place, and method of delivery or performance (see subpart 11.4, Delivery or Performance Schedules, and 47.301-1).>

data.entity_name: Tiered evaluation of small business offers
data.content:
Tiered evaluation of small business offers
A process used in the evaluation of offers for multiple-award contracts, which may be subject to statutory authority.

Multiple-award contracts
The tiered evaluation process is applicable to multiple-award contracts and requires statutory authority.

Section G
<(g) Section G, Contract administration data. Include any required accounting and appropriation data and any required contract administration information or instructions other than those on the solicitation form. Include a statement that the offeror should include the payment address in the proposal, if it is different from that shown for the offeror.>

13 CFR 125.2
The tiered evaluation process is referenced in 13 CFR 125.2, which pertains to the evaluation of offers.

Section H
<(h) Section H, Special contract requirements. Include a clear statement of any special contract requirements that are not included in Section I, Contract clauses, or in other sections of the uniform contract format.>

Evaluation factors and significant subfactors
Key areas of importance and emphasis to be considered in the source selection decision; support meaningful comparison and discrimination between and among competing proposals.

Award decision
The award decision is based on evaluation factors and significant subfactors that are tailored to the acquisition.

Price or cost to the Government
The contracting officer may choose not to include price or cost as an evaluation factor for award when a solicitation-has an estimated value above the simplified acquisition threshold; will result in multiple-award contracts (see subpart 16.5 ) that are for the same or similar services; and States that the Government intends to make an award to each and all qualifying offerors (see 2.101).
data.entity_name: Price or cost to the Government

data.content:
Agency acquisition officials
The evaluation factors and significant subfactors that apply to an acquisition and their relative importance, are within the broad discretion of agency acquisition officials, subject to the following requirements:

Qualifying offerors
The contracting officer may choose not to include price or cost as an evaluation factor for award when a solicitation-has an estimated value above the simplified acquisition threshold; will result in multiple-award contracts (see subpart 16.5 ) that are for the same or similar services; and States that the Government intends to make an award to each and all qualifying offerors (see 2.101).

15.306
A numerical value used in the context of exchanges with offerors after receipt of proposals.

637(d)(4)(G)(ii)
A provision in the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) regarding past performance evaluation factors for acquisitions.

637(d)(4)(G)(i)
A provision in the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) regarding small business subcontracting participation in the evaluation factor.

FAR 7.108(b)
A method in the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) for evaluating offers that include telecommuting.

10 U.S.C. 3206(b)(1)
A section of the United States Code that requires the clear statement of factors affecting contract award in the solicitation.

41 U.S.C. 3306(b)(1)
A section of the United States Code that requires the clear statement of factors affecting contract award in the solicitation.

15.204-5 (c)
A section of the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) that describes the general approach for evaluating past performance information.

Simplified acquisition threshold
The contracting officer may choose not to include price or cost as an evaluation factor for award when a solicitation-has an estimated value above the simplified acquisition threshold; will result in multiple-award contracts (see subpart 16.5 ) that are for the same or similar services; and States that the Government intends to make an award to each and all qualifying offerors (see 2.101).<SEP>The contracting officer may choose not to include price or cost as an evaluation factor for award when a solicitation-has an estimated

Communications with offerors before establishment of the competitive range
relationship between a numerical value and the process of establishing a competitive range in government procurement.<SEP>limitation on the scope of communications with offerors in the context of establishing a competitive range.<SEP>condition for holding communications with offerors based on past performance information.
data.entity_name: Communications with offerors before establishment of the competitive range

data.content:
Relationship
Significantly more important than cost or price<

Uniform contract format
A standardized format for preparing solicitations and contracts as outlined in Table 15-1 of this subsection.

Types of negotiated acquisition
Abstract ideas related to the different environments in which acquisitions are conducted, specifically sole source and competitive acquisitions.

Sole source acquisitions
Solicitation tailoring<|# Query: Q is a step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step step

Section J
A section that lists attachments, including their title, date, and number of pages, with possible cross-references to other sections.

Part
A section that prescribes policies and procedures for competitive and noncompetitive negotiated acquisitions.

List of attachments
Section J references the List of attachments with details such as title, date, and number of pages.

15.301
<relationship_description>

Sealed Bidding
Sealed bidding is an alternative to the negotiated procedures described in this part.

Negotiated Contract
A contract awarded using negotiated procedures is governed by the policies and procedures of this part.

data.entity_name: Exchanges with industry before receipt of proposals
data.content:
Exchanges with industry before receipt of proposals
Encouraged exchanges of information among all interested parties from the earliest identification of a requirement through receipt of proposals, consistent with procurement integrity requirements.

data.entity_name: Lowest price technically acceptable source selection process
data.content:
Lowest price technically acceptable source selection process
A process used in procurement to select the proposal with the lowest evaluated price that meets or exceeds the acceptability standards for non-cost factors.

Agencies
Other participants

Interests
Encouraged exchanges of information among all interested parties to improve understanding of Government requirements and industry capabilities, thereby allowing potential offerors to judge whether or how they can satisfy the Government’s requirements, and enhancing the Government’s ability to obtain quality supplies and services, including construction, at reasonable prices, and increase efficiency in proposal preparation, proposal evaluation, negotiation, and contract award.

Program manager
Other participants

Adverse Past Performance Information
Information regarding past performance that has not been previously addressed by the offeror.

15.101-2
Lowest price technically acceptable source selection process is addressed in 15.101-2.

Competitive Range
The adverse past performance information is addressed and evaluated to determine if a proposal should be placed in the competitive range.

Offeror
The adverse past performance information is addressed and evaluated, and may be considered in rating proposals for the purpose of establishing the competitive range.<SEP>The adverse past performance information to which the offeror has not previously had an opportunity to comment.

Proposal Deficiencies, Material Omissions, Technical or Cost Elements
Such communications shall not be used to cure proposal deficiencies or material omissions, materially alter the technical or cost elements of the proposal, and/or otherwise revise the proposal.<SEP>Such communications may be used to enhance Government understanding of proposals, allow reasonable interpretation of the proposal, or facilitate the Government’s evaluation process.<SEP>Such communications may be considered in rating proposals for the purpose of establishing the competitive range.
data.entity_name: Proposal Deficiencies, Material Omissions, Technical or Cost Elements

data.content:
Deficiency
A material failure of a proposal to meet a Government requirement or a combination of significant weaknesses in a proposal that increases the risk of unsuccessful contract performance to an unacceptable level.

Proposal Interpretation
Such communications may be used to enhance Government understanding of proposals, allow reasonable interpretation of the proposal, or facilitate the Government’s evaluation process.<SEP>Such communications may be used to address ambiguities in the proposal or other concerns (e.g., perceived deficiencies, weaknesses, errors, omissions, or mistakes).

Proposal revision
A "significant weakness" in the proposal is a flaw that appreciably increases the risk of unsuccessful contract performance.

Efficiency Purposes
The competitive range may be further reduced for purposes of efficiency pursuant to paragraph (c)(2) of this section.

Proposal modification
A proposal modification is a change made to a proposal before the solicitation closing date and time, or made in response to an amendment, or made to correct a mistake at any time before award.

Significant weakness
A "significant weakness" in the proposal is a flaw that appreciably increases the risk of unsuccessful contract performance.

Weakness
A "significant weakness" in the proposal is a flaw that appreciably increases the risk of unsuccessful contract performance.

increases
A "significant weakness" in the proposal is a flaw that appreciably increases the risk of unsuccessful contract performance.

Past performance
If the contracting officer elects to consider past performance as an evaluation factor, it shall be evaluated in accordance with 15.305.

Proposal Revision
Such communications may be used to address ambiguities in the proposal or other concerns (e.g., perceived deficiencies, weaknesses, errors, omissions, or mistakes).

Small Business Administration
If the contracting officer determines that a small business’ past performance is not acceptable, the matter shall be referred to the Small Business Administration for a Certificate of Competency determination.

Tradeoffs
Tradeoffs are not permitted.

Exchanges
Exchanges may occur (see 15.306).

data.entity_name: John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019
data.content:
John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019
Except for DoD, in accordance with section 880 of the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019 (Pub. L. 115-232, 41 U.S.C.).<SEP>Legislation that authorizes the Department of Defense appropriations for the fiscal year 2019.

Handling proposals and information
An abstract idea related to the management and processing of proposals and information in a procurement context.

41 U.S.C. 3701
The section 880 of the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019 provides the legal basis for the lowest price technically acceptable source selection process.

Department of Defense
The Department of Defense is an agency that may use the lowest price technically acceptable source selection process as authorized by the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019.

lowest price technically acceptable source selection process
The Department of Defense is an agency that may use the lowest price technically acceptable source selection process as authorized by the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019.
data.entity_name: lowest price technically acceptable source selection process

data.content:
Request for information (RFI)
The RFI process involves the receipt and safeguarding of proposals and information.

Contracting Office
The contracting office responsible for managing proposals, modifications, revisions, or withdrawals.

Late Proposal
A proposal that is submitted after the deadline.

Amendment number and date
Amendment details are described in the change being made.

Number of pages
Amendment details are described in the change being made.

Subpart
A section that describes acquisition processes and techniques for designing competitive acquisition strategies.

oral presentations
Occurrences, incidents, ceremonies, meetings that require offerors to present information orally as part of a source selection process.

source selection decision
Abstract ideas, theories, principles, beliefs that are the outcome of evaluating oral presentations.

oral presentation(s)
Occurrences, incidents, ceremonies, meetings that are part of the source selection process and may include discussions.

oral presentation(s) record
Quantitative or structured information that documents what the Government relied upon in making the source selection decision.

Description of the change being made
The number of pages is related to the description of the change being made in the amendment.
data.entity_name: Description of the change being made

data.content:
oral presentation(s) record method
Procedures, techniques, algorithms, workflows that document the Government's reliance on oral presentations.

data.entity_name: oral presentation(s) record level of detail
data.content:
oral presentation(s) record level of detail
Quantitative or structured information that specifies the level of detail in documenting oral presentations.

data.entity_name: oral presentation(s) record incorporation by reference
data.content:
oral presentation(s) record incorporation by reference
Procedures, techniques, algorithms, workflows that are not permitted for oral presentations.

None
None

oral presentation(s) discussions
Occurrences, incidents, ceremonies, meetings that may occur during oral presentations and are subject to specific regulations.

oral presentation(s) discussions compliance
Procedures, techniques, algorithms, workflows that the Government must follow during discussions in oral presentations.

Government point of contact and phone number
The number of pages is related to the amendment details which include the government point of contact and phone number.<SEP>The number of pages
data.entity_name: Government point of contact and phone number

data.content:
Revision to solicitation closing date, if applicable
The number of pages is related to the amendment details which include the revision to the solicitation closing date.
data.entity_name: Revision to solicitation closing date, if applicable

data.content:
Requests for Proposals
Requests for proposals are used in negotiated acquisitions to communicate Government requirements to prospective contractors and to solicit proposals.

OMB Circular A-76
OMB Circular A-76 studies may be issued as Requests for Proposals.

Electronic Commerce
Electronic commerce may be used to issue Requests for Proposals and to receive proposals, modifications, and revisions.

proposed acquisition
A business transaction that is being considered for purchase or merger.

materials distributed
When conducting a presolicitation or preproposal conference, materials distributed at the conference should be made available to all potential offerors, upon request.

specific information
When specific information about a proposed acquisition is disclosed to potential offerors, it must be made available to the public as soon as practicable, but no later than the next general release of information, to avoid creating an unfair competitive advantage.

Section K
Solicitation provisions that require representations, certifications, or the submission of other information by offerors.

presolicitation or preproposal conference
When conducting a presolicitation or preproposal conference, materials distributed at the conference should be made available to all potential offerors, upon request.

Section L
Solicitation provisions and other information and instructions not required elsewhere to guide offerors or respondents in preparing proposals or responses to requests for information.

Section M
Evaluation factors for award; contracting officer shall insert one of the phrases in 15.304 (e).

potential offerors
When specific information about a proposed acquisition is disclosed to one or more potential offerors, that information must be made available to the public as soon as practicable, but no later than the next general release of information, in order to avoid creating an unfair competitive advantage.

confidential business strategy
Information provided to a potential offeror in response to its request must not be disclosed if doing so would reveal the potential offeror’s confidential business strategy, and is protected under 3.104 or subpart 24.2.

Contracting Officers
Contracting officers may issue Requests for Proposals and/or authorize receipt of proposals, modifications, or revisions by facsimile.

potential offeror
Information provided to a potential offeror in response to its request must not be disclosed if doing so would reveal the potential offeror’s confidential business strategy, and is protected under 3.104 or subpart 24.2.<SEP>Information provided to a potential offeror in response to its request must not be disclosed if doing so would reveal the potential offeror’s confidential business strategy, and is protected

Government Requirements
Requests for proposals communicate Government requirements to prospective contractors.

data.entity_name: American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
data.content:
American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
Legislation that provided funds for various projects and programs in the United States.<SEP>A law enacted to stimulate the economy.

bilateral contract modification
The contracting officer must specify the applicability of Alternate I to a bilateral contract modification that will use funds appropriated or otherwise made available by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009.

Terms and Conditions
The solicitation may authorize offerors to propose alternative terms and conditions.

Anticipated Terms and Conditions
The solicitation may authorize offerors to propose alternative terms and conditions.<SEP>The solicitation may

task- or delivery-order contract
The contracting officer must specify the task or delivery orders to which Alternate I applies in a task- or delivery-order contract in which not all orders will use funds appropriated or otherwise made available by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009.

Governments Requirement

step step

cost-reimbursement contracts
The contracting officer shall use the clause with its Alternate II for cost-reimbursement contracts with State and local Governments, educational institutions, and other nonprofit organizations.

Standard Form 33
A form used to issue RFPs and RFIs.

Standard Form 30
Amend solicitation of negotiated contracts.<SEP>Solicitation of negotiated contracts.

Comptroller General
The contracting officer shall use the clause with its Alternate III when the head of the agency has waived the examination of records by the Comptroller General in accordance with 25.1001.

Optional Form 308
With each request for proposal.<SEP>Issue RFPs and RFIs.<SEP>RFPs and RFIs.

Optional Form 17
With each request for proposal.

facsimile proposals
The contracting officer shall insert the provision at 52.215-5, Facsimile Proposals, in solicitations if facsimile proposals are authorized (see 15.203 (d)).

solicitations
The contracting officer shall insert the clause at 52.215-8, Order of Precedence-Uniform Contract Format, in solicitations and contracts using the format at 15.204.

Master Solicitation
The contracting officer issues solicitations to potential sources and may use a master solicitation for negotiated acquisitions.

Policies and Procedures
The master solicitation is issued in accordance with the policies and procedures in 5.102 , 19.202-4 , and part  6 .<SEP>A master solicitation may be used for negotiated acquisitions.

Standard Form 308
Solicitation of negotiated contracts.

data.entity_name: Acceptable evidence to establish the time of receipt at the Government installation
data.content:
Acceptable evidence to establish the time of receipt at the Government installation
Includes the time/date stamp of that installation on the proposal wrapper, other documentary evidence of receipt maintained by the installation, or oral testimony or statements of Government personnel.

Government installation
time of receipt

Proposal evaluation
An assessment of the proposal and the offeror’s ability to perform the prospective contract successfully, conducted by an agency to evaluate competitive proposals and assess their relative qualities based on specified factors and subfactors, using various rating methods or combinations thereof, and documenting the relative strengths, deficiencies, significant weaknesses, and risks in the contract file.

Cost or price evaluation
Proposal evaluation includes the cost or price evaluation as one of its components, which normally establishes price reasonableness and may include a cost analysis in limited situations to establish the reasonableness of the offeror's price.

Section I
The contracting officer is responsible for including the necessary clauses in Section I of the contract.

Past performance evaluation
Past performance information is considered one indicator of an offeror’s ability to perform the contract successfully, with the contracting officer documenting the evaluation.

Solicitation provisions
Provisions related to contracting by negotiation, including the insertion of specific provisions and clauses in competitive solicitations and contracts.

Instructions to Offerors-Competitive Acquisition
A provision that provides instructions to offerors in competitive acquisitions.

52.215-1
A provision titled "Instructions to Offerors-Competitive Acquisition" used in competitive solicitations.

Competitive Acquisition
A process of awarding contracts without discussions.

52.215-2
A clause titled "Audit and Records-Negotiation" used in solicitations and contracts.

Audit and Records-Negotiation
A clause titled "Audit and Records-Negotiation" related to audit and records requirements.

Pub. L. 111-5
Public Law 111-5, also known as the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009.
